message:
fix(mattermost): persist trimmed auth values back into stored config

The configure() method was trimming base_url, token, and credential_id
for validation but not writing the normalized values back into the
config struct. This meant the stored config could contain leading/
trailing whitespace that already passed validation, causing subtle
mismatches between what was validated and what later code reads from
config.

Changes:
- Use a local `base_url` variable for trimming, then clone the trimmed
  value back into config.base_url via clone_into().
- After the auth match resolves which mode to use, write the trimmed
  token/credential_id back into config.token and config.credential_id
  (and set the unused one to None) so the stored config is always clean.
- Add test configure_normalizes_trimmed_auth_config that verifies
  whitespace-padded base_url and token are trimmed, and a
  whitespace-only credential_id is treated as None.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
